title: T-001 ‚Äî Lock finance permission enum (fin.*.* baseline for 10-FINANCE, ¬ß2.3 RBAC matrix)
prompt:
# T-001 ‚Äî Lock finance permission enum

PRD: docs/prd/10-FINANCE-PRD.md ¬ß2.3 RBAC role matrix; ¬ß5.3 21 CFR Part 11 e-signature; ¬ß12.6 FIN-006 DLQ ops UI.
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Append an FINANCE_CORE permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering every `fin.*.*` capability mandated by PRD ¬ß2.3 role matrix (Finance Manager, Finance Viewer, Production Manager, Plant Director, Admin, Owner, Integration Ops, Auditor) plus DLQ ops + e-signature flows. Export `ALL_FINANCE_CORE_PERMISSIONS`. Per Settings T-001 exemplar the model is flat dot-namespaced strings matching the regex `^fin\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`.

## Implementation contract
1. Append, ONLY at end of the `Permission` const object (do not reorder existing entries), the 14 finance strings listed below, each with a JSDoc comment citing the PRD ¬ß2.3 row OR ¬ß12.6 action it grants.
2. Export `ALL_FINANCE_CORE_PERMISSIONS` as a `readonly Permission[]` array literal so vitest can iterate.
3. Do NOT touch any other module's permission set; do NOT add `fa.*` aliases.
4. CODEOWNERS keeps `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` architect-locked (no change needed if already present).

## Permission strings to add (14)
- `fin.standard_cost.create`     ‚Äî Finance Manager + Owner draft a standard cost (¬ß2.3 row 1)
- `fin.standard_cost.approve`    ‚Äî Finance Manager + Owner approve via SHA-256 e-signature (¬ß2.3 row 2, ¬ß5.3)
- `fin.standard_cost.supersede`  ‚Äî Finance Manager + Owner end-date approved record (¬ß11.2 V-FIN-STD-07)
- `fin.standard_cost.bulk_import` ‚Äî Finance Manager + Admin trigger MODAL-04 (¬ß8.4 FIN-019)
- `fin.wo_cost.view`             ‚Äî Finance Manager + Viewer + Prod Mgr (own line) + Plant Director + Admin (¬ß2.3 row 3)
- `fin.wo_cost.recalculate`      ‚Äî Finance Manager + Owner re-run recursive CTE (¬ß9.2)
- `fin.variance.note`            ‚Äî Finance Manager + Prod Mgr (own WO) (¬ß2.3 row 4, ¬ß11.5)
- `fin.cost_center.admin`        ‚Äî Finance Manager + Admin + Owner CRUD cost_centers + GL mappings (¬ß2.3 row 5)
- `fin.currency.admin`           ‚Äî Finance Manager + Admin manage currencies + manual FX (¬ß2.3 row 5 + row 7)
- `fin.period.lock`              ‚Äî P1 stub gated by feature flag; Finance Manager + Owner only (¬ß8.4 FIN-020 + OQ-FIN-13)
- `fin.dlq.replay`               ‚Äî Finance Manager + Admin (integration_ops) + Owner (¬ß2.3 row 6 + ¬ß12.6)
- `fin.dlq.resolve`              ‚Äî Finance Manager + Admin (integration_ops) + Owner (¬ß12.6)
- `fin.audit.export`             ‚Äî Finance Manager + Viewer + Admin + Owner + auditor_readonly (¬ß2.3 row 8, ¬ß5.2)
- `fin.settings.write`           ‚Äî Finance Manager + Admin update finance_settings (¬ß14.1 L2 variation per-tenant config)

## Files in scope
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- Given the enum file is read, when the export is parsed, then it exposes exactly these 14 new strings: `fin.standard_cost.create`, `fin.standard_cost.approve`, `fin.standard_cost.supersede`, `fin.standard_cost.bulk_import`, `fin.wo_cost.view`, `fin.wo_cost.recalculate`, `fin.variance.note`, `fin.cost_center.admin`, `fin.currency.admin`, `fin.period.lock`, `fin.dlq.replay`, `fin.dlq.resolve`, `fin.audit.export`, `fin.settings.write`.
- Given each new permission key, when matched against the regex `^fin\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, then all match and no duplicates exist across the full `Permission` map.
- Given `ALL_FINANCE_CORE_PERMISSIONS` is exported, when iterated, then `.length === 14` and every entry is typed `Permission`.
- Given a pre-existing entry (e.g. `ORG_ACCESS_ADMIN`, `SETTINGS_ORG_READ`), when the file is diffed against HEAD, then no existing string is renamed, reordered, or deleted.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with a `describe('finance permissions')` block asserting the four ACs above.
2. RED command: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac vitest run permissions.test.ts` ‚Äî must fail before code change.
3. Implement: append 14 entries + `ALL_FINANCE_CORE_PERMISSIONS` export.
4. Re-run vitest ‚Äî must pass. Then `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not remove or rename any existing `Permission.*` entry (ESLint enum-lock guard from Foundation T-046 will fail the build if you do).
- Do not invent new dotted segments outside the `^fin\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` regex (e.g. `fin.standardCost.create` camelCase is forbidden).
- Do not introduce `LegacyPermissionAlias` rows for finance ‚Äî finance is greenfield, no legacy alias needed.
- Do not add `fin.report.*` here ‚Äî reporting lives in 12-REPORTING module; that module owns its own enum delta.
- Do not commit secrets / vault keys / D365 credentials.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED‚ÜíGREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Server Actions. Primitives in `packages/ui`. RBAC enum is the single SoT for permissions.

pipeline_inputs.description: Lock 14 fin.*.* permission strings (P0 RBAC foundation for module 10-FINANCE per PRD ¬ß2.3 RBAC matrix).
pipeline_inputs.details: Append ONLY the 14 listed strings; preserve existing entries verbatim. Each entry has a JSDoc comment citing PRD ¬ß2.3 row or ¬ß12.6 action. Export ALL_FINANCE_CORE_PERMISSIONS readonly array. Follow Settings T-001 pattern exactly (regex check + uniqueness test + array length test). This task blocks every downstream finance task because RBAC checks reference these enum constants.

Do not create LegacyPermissionAlias rows for finance

Run pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint after change.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: 02-settings T-001 locks the permissions.enum CODEOWNERS surface; finance fin.*.* strings must be appended via the same Permission registry without colliding with the SETTINGS_CORE group.
02-settings T-002 co-owns the permissions.enum extension surface (SETTINGS_DATA group); finance enum strings layer on top of the same file.
